sector: Utilities — Independent Power Producer (merchant nuclear + gas/geothermal)
bull_thesis: CEG is the single best-positioned firm-power supplier to the AI build-out and the closest thing to a 'picks-and-shovels toll' on hyperscaler compute. The thesis rests on irreplaceable scarcity: 32.4 GW of NRC-licensed, already-built, carbon-free nuclear baseload that no competitor can permit-and-build inside a hyperscaler's 2027-2029 window. Two marquee 20-year PPAs prove the model is repeatable at premium economics — Microsoft/Crane (835 MW, ~2028 restart, $1B DOE loan guarantee, FERC PJM-rule waiver granted 2026-06-01) and Meta/Clinton (1,121 MW, signed 2025-06-03, commences June 2027) — with a third hyperscaler deal described by the CEO as in the 'late innings.' The $16.4B Calpine acquisition (closed 2026-01-07, ~$2/sh accretive) bolts ~26 GW of dispatchable gas + the Geysers geothermal onto the nuclear fleet, taking CEG to ~55 GW and creating a sellable '24/7 firm power' product (carbon-free baseload + gas peaking) that is exactly what data centers need to never go dark. Two independent earnings levers compound: long-dated PPAs at premium prices AND a structurally tightening PJM capacity market as load outgrows supply. Management affirmed FY26 adj-EPS guidance of $11-12 (vs ~$9.05-9.45 FY25) and beat Q1'26 (adj EPS $2.74 vs $2.56 cons) through a complex integration quarter — high execution credibility. Forward FCF guide is a step-change: $8.4B combined 2026-27 and $11.5-13B 2028-29. Conservative balance sheet (~$5.9B net debt base vs Vistra ~$19.6B) funded the deal with headroom. Free SMR/advanced-reactor optionality at Clinton. CEG is a quality compounder riding a multi-year electrification tailwind, not a cyclical.
bear_thesis: CEG is the most-discovered name in POWER-GEN and the AI-power optionality is largely already in the price. It trades at ~25x forward P/E (+49% vs the IPP peer median ~17x) and ~13x forward EV/EBITDA, with a forward FCF yield of only ~3.8% vs Vistra's ~7.2% — minimal margin of safety. The load-bearing catalysts are essentially FIRED: Microsoft/Crane, Meta/Clinton and the Calpine close have all happened and re-rated the stock; what's left is a future-dated FCF inflection (2027-2029) that is real but unproven in cash. Trailing reality lags the narrative badly: FY25 free cash flow was only ~$1.3B against an ~$86B market cap (FCF yield <2% trailing), and FY25 net margin actually FELL ~6.8pts to 9.1% on commodity/ZEC/outage volatility — merchant earnings are still lumpy and weather/outage-exposed. The entire thesis rides the SAME single shared macro factor — AI-capex durability — as the rest of the scale-out basket, so resolution-driver independence is near zero; an AI-capex air-pocket or monetization scare compresses CEG's premium multiple faster than its asset base. Adding ~26 GW of fossil gas dilutes the carbon-free premium and invites EBITDA re-rating at a lower gas multiple. Co-location / behind-the-meter regulatory framework (the Talen ISA precedent) remains unsettled despite the Crane waiver. You are paying a full premium for an already-known story whose cash proof is years out.
sop_bull: $330-345/sh. Whole-company intrinsic on forward power (post-Calpine, full PPA ramp). Apply ~28x to mid-point FY27 normalized adj-EPS of ~$12.5 (Calpine fully synergized + Meta/Clinton commenced + capacity-price uplift) = ~$350 on earnings power, less a small premium-fatigue haircut. Cross-check via FCF: 2028-29 guide $11.5-13B FCF; at a ~3.3% FCF yield (premium-asset, infrastructure-grade) the ~$12B run-rate supports ~$365B EV; netting ~$16B post-deal net debt leaves ~$110-115/sh of equity per... (re-based to ~315M shares) ~$310-340/sh. SMR/Clinton advanced-reactor optionality and a third hyperscaler PPA add a further unpriced call worth ~$15-25/sh. Parts: nuclear fleet (premium PPA-contracted baseload) ~70% of value, ~$230/sh; Calpine gas+geothermal (dispatchable firm-power complement) ~25%, ~$85/sh; growth optionality (SMR + next PPA) ~$25/sh. Bull SoP ~$340/sh.
sop_bear: $200-215/sh. Strip the premium and value on demonstrated, not promised, economics. Re-rate to ~17x the IPP peer-median forward multiple on FY26 mid-guide adj-EPS ~$11.5 = ~$195/sh. Cross-check on trailing FCF (~$1.3B FY25) — at even a generous 4.5% yield that supports only ~$29B equity (~$92/sh), underscoring how forward-loaded the case is. Value the gas EBITDA at a lower fossil multiple than nuclear (re-rate risk). Parts: nuclear baseload at a de-rated ~16-18x ~$160/sh; Calpine gas at ~7x EBITDA (fossil discount) ~$45/sh; zero credit for unfired SMR/third-PPA optionality. Apply a haircut for AI-capex-durability risk and merchant-margin volatility. Bear SoP ~$205/sh.
sop_breakdown: Base case blends the demonstrated power-earnings franchise with a partial (not full) credit for the forward PPA/Calpine ramp. Nuclear fleet (32.4 GW, premium long-dated PPA-contracted + capacity-market uplift): ~$185/sh — the durable, irreplaceable core, valued at ~20-21x its share of normalized EPS (a deserved premium to the IPP median for licensed scarcity, but below the bull's 28x). Calpine gas + Geysers geothermal (~26 GW dispatchable firm-power complement): ~$60/sh at ~8-9x EBITDA (a blended multiple — above pure-fossil, below nuclear, crediting the firm-power synergy). Growth/optionality (third hyperscaler PPA in 'late innings' + Clinton SMR call): ~$15/sh, partially credited since the PPA flywheel is proven repeatable but each leg is future-dated. Sum ~$260/sh. This sits ~5% BELOW the ~$274 spot — i.e., the market is already paying slightly above a fair, partial-credit SoP, consistent with a ~25x multiple +49% over peers.
risk_reward: Poor-to-balanced. Spot ~$274. Upside-to-fair (~$260) is NEGATIVE ~5%; upside-to-bull (~$340) is ~+24% but requires full Calpine synergy + Meta/Clinton commencement + a third PPA + sustained AI-capex, i.e. everything goes right and stays right. Downside-to-bear (~$205) is ~-25% on an AI-capex digestion or premium-compression event. So at spot the asymmetry is roughly 1:1 to slightly negative (24% up vs 25% down, with the base case already below spot). The favorable entry is on a washout toward the ~$205-230 zone, not here.
catalyst_status: FIRED. The three load-bearing catalysts have all occurred and re-rated the stock: (1) Calpine acquisition CLOSED 2026-01-07 ($16.4B, ~$2/sh accretive); (2) Meta/Clinton 20-year PPA SIGNED 2025-06-03 (1,121 MW, commences June 2027); (3) Microsoft/Crane restart de-risked — $1B DOE loan guarantee (Nov 2025) + FERC PJM-rule waiver granted 2026-06-01 (restart still future-dated to ~2028). Remaining live items are SOFT_EXTENDED: a third hyperscaler PPA the CEO called 'late innings' (undated, unnamed, single-binary) and Clinton SMR/advanced-reactor optionality (only 'evaluating regulatory pathways'; ESP expires 2027-03-15, no capital committed). Net: the re-rate is largely spent; what remains is execution/commencement of already-announced deals plus one unverifiable pending award. Defensive/arb profile, not asymmetric special-sit.
peer_comps_note: True peers are the merchant power / nuclear-IPP scale-out cohort, NOT regulated utilities. Live multiples (verified June 2026): CEG forward P/E ~25x (gurufocus 25.12 as of 2026-05-27, +49% vs the IPP industry-median ~16.8x), forward EV/EBITDA ~13x, forward FCF yield ~3.8%, net debt ~$5.9B base (pre/early Calpine). Vistra (VST): forward P/E ~18x, EV/EBITDA ~10x, FCF yield ~7.2%, net debt ~$19.6B (FY26 adj-EBITDA guide $6.8-7.6B) — cheaper and more cash-generative but far more levered and gas-weighted, less of a pure-nuclear-scarcity story. Talen (TLN) and NRG are the other comps (the Talen/Susquehanna co-location ISA is the key behind-the-meter regulatory precedent for the whole group); live multiples for TLN/NRG were not cleanly retrievable this session — flagged as a gap. RANK within the scale-out POWER-GEN layer: CEG is #1 on AI-capex exposure / quality / nuclear-scarcity moat and #1 on balance-sheet conservatism, but #LAST on valuation (richest multiple) and on FCF yield (lowest). It is the highest-quality, most-discovered, most-expensive name in the basket — you pay up for the best asset.
role_in_scaleout: CEG is the prime firm-power generator for the build-out: it owns the scarce, NRC-licensed nuclear baseload (plus, post-Calpine, dispatchable gas) that lets it sell '24/7 firm' carbon-free power directly to hyperscalers on 20-year PPAs — the literal energy a data center must have before any chip, optic, or transformer matters. Demand is highly durable (20-year contracts, irreplaceable licensed assets, tightening PJM capacity market) so long as AI-capex itself persists; the asset's terminal value is not at risk, only its premium multiple.
consensus_delta: There is NO favorable consensus delta here — the street is largely RIGHT and already positioned. Consensus already prices CEG as a premium AI-infrastructure asset (~25x forward, +49% vs IPP peers, ~3.8% FCF yield); the marquee PPAs and the Calpine accretion are in every model. If anything the contrarian read cuts AGAINST the bull: the gap the market may be UNDER-appreciating is the risk side — that trailing FCF (~$1.3B FY25) and falling FY25 net margin (-6.8pts to 9.1%) sit far below a narrative that has fully discounted 2028-2029 cash. The expectations-arbitrage is therefore negative-to-neutral: you are buying a correctly-identified great asset at a price that already embeds the optionality, with the residual surprise risk skewed toward AI-capex-durability disappointment rather than upside. The one genuine non-consensus lever is the tightening PJM capacity market as a SECOND earnings engine independent of any single PPA — but that too is increasingly in sell-side notes.
valley_of_death: Near-term (3-9 month) risks are not balance-sheet — CEG is investment-grade with deal headroom — but multiple/sentiment-driven. (1) AI-capex air-pocket: a single hyperscaler capex guide-down or AI-monetization scare (the regime's named tripwire; AI-capex ~40% of 2026 S&P EPS growth) would de-gross the entire scale-out cohort and compress CEG's premium multiple fastest, since its valuation is most stretched. (2) Merchant-margin volatility: Q1'26 already showed ZEC pricing drag, extra nuclear refueling-outage days, and Winter Storm Fern cost-to-serve — outage/weather noise can pressure a quarter and rattle a richly-priced stock. (3) Calpine integration slippage or a gas-EBITDA re-rate (market assigning fossil generation a lower multiple) would mechanically lower the SoP. (4) Co-location / behind-the-meter regulatory reversal at FERC (the unresolved Talen-ISA framework) would cloud the data-center-direct PPA model. None of these impairs terminal value, but each can drive a 15-25% drawdown from a +49%-premium starting multiple before any catalyst pays off.
positioning_washout: No classic forced-seller / yield-base-liquidation dynamic — CEG is a growth/AI-momentum holding, not a high-yield name, so there is no dividend-cut-driven mechanical exit. The washout risk is the OPPOSITE: CEG is a crowded, momentum-and-passive-heavy long inside the AI-capex factor (the regime flags BofA FMS cash at 3.9% sell-signal and AI-capex as the single dominant factor under the tape). A de-grossing event would hit CEG via factor-unwind and momentum-reversal selling, not a yield rotation — i.e., it is on the wrong side of a positioning washout, a candidate to be SOLD in a flush, which is exactly when its risk/reward would improve. Wait for that flush rather than buy the crowd.
forcing_function: The hard dates that could re-rate are mostly behind us (Calpine close 2026-01-07; Meta/Clinton signing 2025-06-03; FERC Crane waiver 2026-06-01). Forward forcing functions: (a) announcement/signing of the 'late-innings' third hyperscaler PPA (undated — would extend the flywheel and is the main positive surprise left); (b) Crane Clean Energy Center restart on schedule in ~2028 (the binding execution proof of the Microsoft deal); (c) Meta/Clinton PPA commencement June 2027 (cash-flow turn-on); (d) Clinton ESP decision before its 2027-03-15 expiry (SMR optionality). Most are 2027-2028-dated — too far to anchor a near-term entry — and the nearest (the third PPA) is unverifiable/undated. There is no imminent, dated re-rating catalyst that isn't already priced.
moderator_conclusion: CEG is the highest-quality asset in POWER-GEN and arguably the cleanest firm-power toll on the entire AI build-out — a genuinely WIDE regulatory-license + scale-cost moat (32.4 GW of irreplaceable licensed nuclear) executing a transformative-but-accretive Calpine deal cleanly, with two proven 20-year hyperscaler PPAs and a tightening capacity market as a second earnings lever. The Interrogator's STRENGTHENING / credibility-4 read is correct on the business. But this is an investment-committee capital-allocation call, not a quality contest, and the regime lens is decisive: the load-bearing catalysts (Calpine close, Meta/Clinton, Microsoft/Crane) are FIRED and the re-rate is spent; the stock trades at ~25x forward (+49% vs IPP peers) with a ~3.8% FCF yield and trailing FCF (~$1.3B) and falling FY25 margins far below a narrative that has fully discounted 2028-2029 cash. My reconciled base-case SoP is ~$260, roughly 5% BELOW the ~$274 spot, with ~1:1-to-negative risk/reward (24% to the bull, 25% to the bear). Critically, CEG fails the regime's resolution-driver-independence test hardest in the basket — its thesis rides the identical AI-capex-durability factor as every other scale-out name, so it adds concentration, not diversification, and it is on the wrong side of a positioning washout. The regime explicitly tells me to PENALIZE already-fired / already-ran catalysts and prize independence — both cut against CEG here. Verdict C / conviction 2: PASS at this price; this is a watch-for-capitulation name, not an entry. Buy the asset on an AI-capex flush toward the ~$205-230 zone, where the premium compresses and the SoP gives real margin of safety. Separately, value_conviction 3: as a pure value-vs-SoP call stripped of catalyst overlay, CEG is fairly-to-slightly-richly priced on a WIDE, non-eroding moat — not a value trap (the terminal asset is durable) but not cheap either, so a neutral 3, capped there by the full multiple rather than by any moat erosion.
interrogator_dossier:
# Forensic Dossier — Constellation Energy (CEG)

*Prepared by the Interrogator for the investment committee. Capital is real. Data: live web (FY2025 results, Q1 2026 earnings call/release, Calpine close, hyperscaler PPA pipeline) as of 2026-06-22. CEG trades on NASDAQ at ~$274, market cap ~$86-89B.*

## 1. NARRATIVE ARC
CEG's story has undergone a deliberate, staged metamorphosis from 'carbon-free merchant nuclear IPP with policy-protected cash flows' to 'the firm-power backbone of the AI build-out.' Phase 1 (2022-2023, post-spin): decarbonization + the IRA nuclear Production Tax Credit floor, ZEC programs, capital return — nuclear as a defensive clean-baseload asset. Phase 2 (2024, the pivot): the Sept-2024 Microsoft / Three Mile Island (Crane Clean Energy Center) 20-year PPA recast the thesis — nuclear became a scarce 24/7 firm power source hyperscalers pay a structural premium for. Phase 3 (2025-2026, scale + firming): the Meta / Clinton 20-year PPA (announced 2025-06-03, 1,121 MW, commences June 2027) proved Microsoft was a template not a one-off; and the $16.4B Calpine acquisition (closed 2026-01-07) bolts ~26 GW of gas + the Geysers geothermal onto the 32.4 GW nuclear fleet (~55 GW total), enabling a '24/7 firm' product blending carbon-free baseload with gas peaking. Drivers introduced: the data-center PPA flywheel, co-location premium, SMR optionality, gas dispatchability. Drivers de-emphasized: pure decarbonization/ESG framing and ZEC reliance (ZEC pricing is now a headwind line-item being replaced by long-dated PPAs at better economics).

## 2. CLAIMS vs FINANCIALS
Calpine accretion: Q1'26 adj EPS $2.74 vs $2.14 Q1'25; mgmt attributes ~$2/sh full-year accretion; FY26 guide $11-12 vs FY25 ~$9.05-9.45 — CORROBORATED, the ~20-30% EPS step-change is real and Calpine-driven. Revenue scaling: Q1'26 rev $11.1B (+64% YoY), FY25 $25.5B (+8.3%) — TRUE but mix/volume-driven (Calpine consolidation), not organic price. FCF: FY25 only ~$1.3B but 2026-27 guide $8.4B combined, 2028-29 $11.5-13B — TENSION: trailing FCF yield <2% on ~$86B cap; the cash thesis is forward-loaded and unproven. Balance sheet: net debt ~$5.9B base vs Vistra ~$19.6B; Calpine added ~$10B+ (EV $26.6B incl debt) — MANAGEABLE off a conservative base, IG. Capital return: 1.2M shares bought at ~$285, $335M since last call, dividend growing — CORROBORATED but small (~0.4% annualized), not the thesis. Demand: 2026 hyperscaler spend '~75% higher than last year,' ~5,000 MW into PJM queue — CORROBORATED by external AI-capex data. The story is RICHER than trailing numbers: FY25 net margin FELL ~6.8pts to 9.1%; the valuation prices the forward cash machine, not the trailing one.

## 3. TONE & CONFIDENCE TRAJECTORY
Rising / offensive. Shift from defensive ('policy floor protects us') to offensive ('we are the scarce asset; hyperscalers compete for our megawatts'). CEO commentary that a major data-center transaction was in the 'late innings' ('I wouldn't discuss it if I didn't think it was viable') signals negotiating from strength. Affirming (not cutting) $11-12 guidance through a complex integration quarter is a confidence tell.

## 4. GUIDANCE CREDIBILITY
High. Affirmed FY26 adj-EPS $11-12 in Q1'26 despite integration complexity, outage timing and Winter Storm Fern drag, and still beat Q1 consensus ($2.74 vs $2.56, +7%). FY25 guide narrowed and delivered. Multi-year FCF guide is specific/dated. Caveat: forward FCF ramp embeds Calpine synergies + PPA commencement (Meta June 2027, Crane 2028) not yet proven in cash. Reliability on what they control: High.

## 5. ANALYST PRESSURE POINTS
'How much is already priced in?' (forward P/E ~25x vs IPP median ~17x, +49%). Co-location / behind-the-meter regulatory risk (Talen/Susquehanna ISA precedent) — partly de-risked by the 2026-06-01 FERC PJM-rule waiver but framework still evolving. Calpine integration + gas-fleet ESG dilution ('is this still a clean-energy premium asset?'). ZEC roll-off timing gaps.

## 6. RED FLAGS / GREEN FLAGS
GREEN: affirmed guide + Q1 beat through the Calpine close; two marquee 20-year PPAs prove repeatable premium demand + a third in 'late innings'; scarcity moat (32.4 GW nuclear irreplaceable on the AI timeline); conservative balance sheet vs peers. RED: trailing FCF thin (~$1.3B FY25) vs ~$86B cap; FY25 net margin fell ~6.8pts to 9.1%; valuation forward P/E ~25x (+49% vs IPP median), minimal MOS; single dominant shared factor — same AI-capex wave as the whole basket, low resolution-driver independence.

## 7. HIDDEN SIGNALS
Terminology migrated from 'zero-emission/carbon-free' to 'firm/24-7/reliable' — repositioning from ESG asset to grid-criticality asset (more durable, less policy-dependent). 'Capacity prices in PJM' now a tailwind line — a second independent earnings lever as load outgrows supply. Calpine gas recoded from 'fossil liability' to 'the dispatchable partner that makes nuclear sellable as firm power' (SoP risk both ways). SMR/advanced-reactor at Clinton is optionality not capital-committed (ESP expires 2027-03-15) — a free call management keeps alive.

## 8. CAPITAL-ALLOCATION VERDICT
Trajectory STRENGTHENING: improving franchise — repeatable premium PPAs, transformative-but-accretive deal executed cleanly, affirmed guidance, tightening capacity market as a second lever; capital allocated offensively from scarcity-driven pricing power. The single thing to weigh before sizing: how much AI-power optionality is already in the ~25x forward multiple (+49% vs IPP peers). CEG is the highest-quality name in POWER-GEN but among the most already-discovered — the stock has run, the marquee catalysts are largely FIRED, and the forward FCF inflection that justifies the multiple is real but future-dated (2027-2029). A quality-compounder-at-full-price problem, not a mispricing. Moat: regulatory-license + scale-cost — 32.4 GW of irreplaceable NRC-licensed baseload no competitor can replicate inside the AI window — structurally WIDE; but returns evidence is mixed (ROIC ~15.5% good, yet FY25 net margin fell ~6.8pts and trailing FCF thin), so realized-return trend is STABLE-to-improving on guidance, not yet visibly widening in the financials. Secular force is a TAILWIND (AI-driven electrification), not a terminal threat; the structural risk is the inverse of most names — not demand erosion but demand-durability dependence: if AI-capex digests, the premium multiple compresses faster than the asset base, but terminal value of the licensed fleet is not impaired. Secular threat: manageable.

CREDIBILITY_SCORE: 4 | TRAJECTORY: STRENGTHENING | MOAT: WIDE | MOAT_TREND: STABLE | SECULAR_THREAT: manageable
skeptic_kill_fact: No kill_fact: every load-bearing figure independently verifies against primary/IR sources, so the bear (PASS/watch-for-capitulation) thesis SURVIVES the kill-check rather than being broken. The only thing that could not be confirmed is the BULL-side 'third hyperscaler PPA in late innings' — undated, unnamed, no primary announcement exists — which is correctly treated as SOFT and is not load-bearing for the bear case.
skeptic_corrections: (1) Market cap understated: debate says ~$86B; live FMP quote shows ~$98.4B at the $274.06 spot (share count higher post-Calpine stock consideration). This makes trailing FCF yield even THINNER (~$1.29B / ~$98B = ~1.3%), strengthening, not weakening, the bear. (2) Net debt base ~$5.26B per FY25 10-K (debate said ~$5.9B) — immaterial, slightly conservative. (3) FERC Crane/TMI waiver granted ~2026-06-04 (transfer of 760 MW interconnection rights from retiring Eddystone), not 2026-06-01 — ~3-day date imprecision, immaterial; substance (waiver granted, restart targeted H2-2027, $1B DOE loan Nov-2025) confirms. (4) Crane restart target is H2-2027 (835 MW), not '~2028' as stated in two places in the debate — modest timeline correction, does not change the cash-is-forward-loaded conclusion. (5) Technical context the debate omits: spot $274.06 sits BELOW the 50-DMA (~$284) and 200-DMA (~$319) and is ~34% off the 52-wk high ($412.70) — i.e. the premium-compression / de-rating the bear warns about is ALREADY underway, reinforcing the 'wait for the flush' stance.
skeptic_evidence: Constellation Energy IR, 'Constellation, Meta Sign 20-Year Deal for Clean, Reliable Nuclear Energy in Illinois' (2025-06-03): 1,121 MW Clinton Clean Energy Center 20-yr PPA, deliveries commence June 2027 — load-bearing PPA #2 CONFIRMED (investors.constellationenergy.com).

Hart Energy / Industrial Info, 'Constellation Completes $16.4B Calpine Deal' (closed 2026-01-07; ~$26.6B total incl ~$12.7B assumed Calpine net debt, ~26 GW gas + Geysers geothermal) — Calpine close CONFIRMED.

Utility Dive, 'Constellation's Three Mile Island nuclear restart gets boost with FERC waiver' (2026-06-04): FERC granted transfer of ~760 MW interconnection rights from retiring Eddystone to Crane; restart targeted H2-2027; backed by $1B DOE loan guarantee (Nov-2025) and 20-yr Microsoft PPA — Crane catalyst CONFIRMED (date corrected to 06-04).

CEG FY2025 results (SEC 8-K / company release): revenue $25.5B (+8%), GAAP net income $2.32B (FELL YoY on MtM/decommissioning), adj EPS $9.39, CFO $4.24B less capex $2.95B = FCF ~$1.29B, net debt ~$5.26B — confirms thin trailing FCF / falling-margin bear core. Live FMP quote 2026-06-18: CEG $274.06, mkt cap ~$98.4B, 50-DMA $284, 200-DMA $319, 52-wk high $412.70 (de-rating already underway).

scale.role: Single best-positioned firm-power supplier — 32.4 GW NRC-licensed nuclear, Meta/Clinton + Microsoft/Crane PPAs, Calpine closed.
scale.rationale: Irreplaceable scarcity (already-built licensed nuclear, the scarcest asset in the layer) and the purest nuclear-IPP toll after VST — a true CORE franchise (~85). Skeptic verdict CONFIRMED_WITH_CORRECTIONS, corrections are footnotes that REINFORCE the wait-stance (mcap higher so FCF yield thinner; de-rating already underway, ~34% off the high, below 50/200-DMA), so a ~8pt haircut. Catalyst FIRED (all three load-bearing events printed), base ~$260 ~5% below spot — seat-on-weakness, the least-redundant nuclear name after VST.
scale.demand_durability: Very high — licensed nuclear under long PPAs; the scarcest asset in the layer.
